title: Iranecio oligolepis
taxonomy: Iranecio oligolepis is classified as a species within the genus Iranecio in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other organisms. The classification of Iranecio oligolepis reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Iranecio oligolepis has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its medicinal uses are not extensively documented. It is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. The plant's exact medicinal applications are limited in available information, but it is thought to have some antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Iranecio oligolepis is often used to treat various health issues, including wounds and skin irritations. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and benefits.

intro: Iranecio oligolepis, also known as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, is a type of herb used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Iranecio oligolepis include iranol, iranolide, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
